copy.title: Tigers edge Rays in slugfest. Brewers demolish Giants. Knicks-Spurs Finals tip tomorrow night.

lead.headline: Rockies steal one in Angels' house, late drama wins it
lead.whatHappened: Colorado walked out of Anaheim with a 9-8 win after the Angels couldn't close it out. The kind of game where one team plays sloppy baseball and still almost wins, but almost doesn't cut it.
lead.whyBullet1: The Angels are supposed to be better than this—losing at home to a Rockies team that's nowhere near playoff contention is the kind of loss that sticks.
lead.whyBullet2: In a weak AL West, these single-run home games matter. You don't gift wins to teams fighting for the same division.
lead.whatToSay: The Angels had every chance to put this away and just couldn't execute down the stretch.

sportsOther: Tigers scraped out a win nobody will remember in two weeks.
Brewers didn't just beat the Giants—they embarrassed them at home.
Rockies survived a one-run game that felt longer than it should.

golf.headline: Campbell, Coody, Fox deadlocked at Memorial; major test begins
golf.whyCare1: Muirfield Village is the toughest track on tour—this week separates pretenders from real contenders heading into summer.
golf.whyCare2: Campbell, Coody, and Fox are all tied after 18 holes, which means no one's found the course's rhythm yet and the field is still wide open.
golf.watchFor: How the lead pack handles Muirfield's greens on day two—this course punishes even small mistakes, so watch for anyone who takes command early or falls apart.
golf.whatToSay: The Memorial always gets weird because nobody shoots low numbers; it's the guy who stays patient and limits damage that wins.

f1.headline: Monaco's the one track where money can't buy you wins.
f1.whyCare1: Monaco strips away raw speed advantage and exposes who actually knows how to drive on the edge.
f1.whyCare2: Championship points matter less here than proving you belong in the elite tier of precision drivers.
f1.watchFor: Qualifying on Saturday — pole position is worth roughly half the race because overtaking here is nearly impossible.
f1.whatToSay: Monaco's where you find out who's genuinely elite and who just has the best car.
